sanskrit: इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः। तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः॥
transliteration: iṣṭān bhogān hi vo devā dāsyante yajña-bhāvitāḥ | tair dattān apradāyaibhyo yo bhuṅkte stena eva saḥ ||
literal_meaning: The demigods, nourished by sacrifice, will indeed bestow upon you the desired enjoyments. One who enjoys what is given by them without offering back is verily a thief.
contextual_meaning: Psychologically, the verse warns that taking from life without reciprocity breeds unconscious guilt and a ‘thief’ self-image, eroding authentic self-esteem.
psychological_state: Moral indebtedness; covert guilt when receiving without giving back.
trigger_scenario: Using open-source software daily but never contributing code, donations, or thanks—then feeling uneasy when asked to give back.
anchor_text: Give back to life what you take, or guilt will steal your peace.
